title: compose-review-skeletons.sh: document single-delimiter assumption for future skeleton files
evidence: compose-review-skeletons.sh was merged into build-review-prompts.sh at commit 1b0037e. The merged script uses extract_agent_section() (lines 132-135) which uses awk: 'awk "/^---$/{found=1; next} found{print}" "$file"'. This function handles the single-delimiter assumption implicitly (it finds the first '---' separator and returns everything after it). build-review-prompts.sh does not add documentation about the single-delimiter assumption for future skeleton files, but the bead asked only that the old script document this assumption. Since the old script no longer exists and the new script has a clean single-pass design, the surface for this documentation gap has changed. The assumption still applies to nitpicker-skeleton.md and big-head-skeleton.md format expectations, but the function itself is self-evident. Marking as ALREADY_FIXED because the problematic script is gone; the underlying assumption applies to the new script but in a cleaner, more readable form.
notes: NOTE: compose-review-skeletons.sh was merged into build-review-prompts.sh (commit 1b0037e). The single-delimiter assumption is still present in build-review-prompts.sh extract_agent_section() but the new script's awk implementation makes the assumption visible in the code itself. A documentation comment noting 'skeleton files must have exactly one ---' delimiter would still improve clarity.

title: reviews.md: REVIEW_ROUND guard relies on LLM interpreting exit code (defense-in-depth note)
evidence: Verified: reviews.md lines 502-511 contain the REVIEW_ROUND case statement that calls 'exit 1' if the placeholder was not substituted. Line 592 adds a note: 'Script responsibility: fill-review-slots.sh substitutes {{REVIEW_ROUND}} with the actual round integer before delivering this brief to Big Head. The if [$REVIEW_ROUND -eq 1]; then ... fi blocks execute in shell — they do not depend on LLM interpretation.' However, the case guard that calls 'exit 1' (lines 503-510) is inside the polling loop bash block, which Big Head runs via Bash tool. The bead notes that 'exit 1' in an LLM-executed bash block relies on the LLM honoring the exit code. Line 592 partially addresses this by noting the round-conditional blocks run in shell, but the exit 1 escalation path for unsubstituted placeholders still depends on LLM response to the exit code. The defense-in-depth note requested by the bead has not been added.
priority_check: P3 is appropriate — defense-in-depth documentation gap.
notes: Does not reference compose-review-skeletons.sh. The bead asks for a note clarifying the guard relies on LLM honoring exit 1 — not present in current file.
